text: The lumbar enchyma was completely subverted.
First of all, we resected the pulmonary ligament and opened the mandestinal pleura anteriorly and posteriorly to isolate the superior and inferior vein.
The first vessel we usually transect is the inferior pulmonary vein using a mechanical stapler inserted from the utility incision.
Then we isolate the superior one and close it with the mechanical stapler inserted from the inferior axis.
Once transected, we noticed a small vein draining the S6 segment, so we had to close it separately.
Then we carefully cleared the pulmonary artery just over the pericardium and transected it using a mechanical stapler inserted from the inferior axis.
Again, we cleaned the bronchus and decided to close it with a TA.
The utility incision was enough to enter that stapler and we were able to obtain a bronchial stamp as short as possible.
A systematic lymphadenectomy was then performed and a bronchial stamp covered with a glue.
